title: Tephroseris kjellmanii
uses: Tephroseris kjellmanii, also known as Kjellman's sericocarp, has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Russia and Asia, for its potential health benefits. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. taxonomy: Tephroseris kjellmanii is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau in China and is also found in parts of the Himalayan Mountains. The classification of Tephroseris kjellmanii includes its placement in the genus Tephroseris, which is often considered to be a synonym of the genus Cremanium.
